coredata.dc:title: Effects of Pressure Evolution on the Decrease in the Capacity of Lithium-Ion Batteries
coredata.prism:copyright: Copyright © 2020 The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.prism:publisher: The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.dc:creator: Zhang, Jun
Kang, Bowen
Luo, Qing
Zou, Shunzhang
coredata.dc:description: External mechanical pressure can affect the cycle life of lithium-ion battery. In this paper, the evolution process of the mechanical pressure that a lithium-ion battery was subjected to during approximately 3000 cycles under the fixed constraint was studied through charge-discharge cycling tests of a lithium-ion battery. The effect of external pressure on battery aging in the late cycle stage was explored through SEM and incremental capacity analysis (ICA). In addition, three groups of comparative experiments, including a rigid constraint, an elastic silicone pad constraint and a spring constraint, were set up to explore the influence of different constraint schemes on battery cycle life. The experimental results showed that the mechanical pressure increased due to the aging of the battery under the fixed constraint, which could cause the anode material of the battery to crack. In the comparative experiment, the capacity decrease rate of the spring compression constraint scheme was 5.13% and 6.17% lower than those of the other two groups. The ohmic resistance increase rate was 8.67% and 12.36% lower than those of the other two groups, and the coulombic efficiency of the spring constraint remained good in the late stage of the cycle. Therefore, the spring constraint scheme can maximize the positive effect of external pressure on lithium-ion batteries by maintaining a relatively stable external pressure. The results presented in this paper have a certain guiding significance for the design of the battery pack.
coredata.dcterms:subject: lithium-ion battery
pressure evolution
aging
decrease in capacity

xocs:meta.xocs:articleinfo: rawtext articleinfo articletitlenorm authfirstinitialnorm authfirstsurnamenorm cid cids contenttype copyright crossmark dateloaded dateloadedtxt datesearch datesort dateupdated dco docsubtype doctype doi eid ewtransactionid hubeid indexeddate issfirst issn issnnorm issuelist itemstage itemtransactionid itemweight oauserlicense openaccess openarchive pg pgfirst pglast pii piinorm pubdateend pubdatestart pubdatetxt pubyr sortorder srctitle srctitlenorm srctype ssids alllist content oa subj tomb volfirst volissue volumelist webpdf webpdfpagecount yearnav affil articletitle auth authfirstini authfull authkeywords authlast primabst ref
xocs:meta.xocs:copyright-line: Copyright © 2020 The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.

xocs:doc.xocs:rawtext: Effects of Pressure Evolution on the Decrease in the Capacity of Lithium-Ion Batteries Jun Zhang, 1 Bowen Kang, 1 â�Ž kbw520@hnu.edu.cn Qing Luo, 2 Shunzhang Zou, 1 1 College of Mechanical and Vehicle Engineering, Hunan University, Changsha 410082, Hunan, China College of Mechanical and Vehicle Engineering Hunan University Changsha Hunan 410082 China College of Mechanical and Vehicle Engineering, Hunan University, Changsha 410082, Hunan, China 2 Hunan Fengyuan Yeshine Kingco New Energy Co., Ltd., Changsha 410100, Hunan, China Hunan Fengyuan Yeshine Kingco New Energy Co., Ltd. Changsha Hunan 410100 China Hunan Fengyuan Yeshine Kingco New Energy Co., Ltd., Changsha 410100, Hunan, China â�Ž Corresponding author: Kang Bowen External mechanical pressure can affect the cycle life of lithium-ion battery. In this paper, the evolution process of the mechanical pressure that a lithium-ion battery was subjected to during approximately 3000 cycles under the fixed constraint was studied through charge-discharge cycling tests of a lithium-ion battery. The effect of external pressure on battery aging in the late cycle stage was explored through SEM and incremental capacity analysis (ICA). In addition, three groups of comparative experiments, including a rigid constraint, an elastic silicone pad constraint and a spring constraint, were set up to explore the influence of different constraint schemes on battery cycle life. The experimental results showed that the mechanical pressure increased due to the aging of the battery under the fixed constraint, which could cause the anode material of the battery to crack. In the comparative experiment, the capacity decrease rate of the spring compression constraint scheme was 5.13% and 6.17% lower than those of the other two groups. The ohmic resistance increase rate was 8.67% and 12.36% lower than those of the other two groups, and the coulombic efficiency of the spring constraint remained good in the late stage of the cycle. Therefore, the spring constraint scheme can maximize the positive effect of external pressure on lithium-ion batteries by maintaining a relatively stable external pressure. The results presented in this paper have a certain guiding significance for the design of the battery pack. Keywords: lithium-ion battery pressure evolution aging decrease in capacity References 1. simple-head.ce:title: Effects of Pressure Evolution on the Decrease in the Capacity of Lithium-Ion Batteries

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: College of Mechanical and Vehicle Engineering, Hunan University, Changsha 410082, Hunan, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: College of Mechanical and Vehicle Engineering

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: College of Mechanical and Vehicle Engineering, Hunan University, Changsha 410082, Hunan, China

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Hunan Fengyuan Yeshine Kingco New Energy Co., Ltd., Changsha 410100, Hunan, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Hunan Fengyuan Yeshine Kingco New Energy Co., Ltd.

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Hunan Fengyuan Yeshine Kingco New Energy Co., Ltd., Changsha 410100, Hunan, China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: External mechanical pressure can affect the cycle life of lithium-ion battery. In this paper, the evolution process of the mechanical pressure that a lithium-ion battery was subjected to during approximately 3000 cycles under the fixed constraint was studied through charge-discharge cycling tests of a lithium-ion battery. The effect of external pressure on battery aging in the late cycle stage was explored through SEM and incremental capacity analysis (ICA). In addition, three groups of comparative experiments, including a rigid constraint, an elastic silicone pad constraint and a spring constraint, were set up to explore the influence of different constraint schemes on battery cycle life. The experimental results showed that the mechanical pressure increased due to the aging of the battery under the fixed constraint, which could cause the anode material of the battery to crack. In the comparative experiment, the capacity decrease rate of the spring compression constraint scheme was 5.13% and 6.17% lower than those of the other two groups. The ohmic resistance increase rate was 8.67% and 12.36% lower than those of the other two groups, and the coulombic efficiency of the spring constraint remained good in the late stage of the cycle. Therefore, the spring constraint scheme can maximize the positive effect of external pressure on lithium-ion batteries by maintaining a relatively stable external pressure. The results presented in this paper have a certain guiding significance for the design of the battery pack.
